title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. pilosum
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. pilosum, also known as rockrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a shrubby perennial herb with clusters of pink or white flowers, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The rockrose plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of rockrose, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate stress, as well as to support cardiovascular health and boost the immune system.
